name: Boiled Pot-Stickers (Shwei Jow)
recipeInstructions:
---------------
WRAPPERS
1 cup All-purpose flour
1/4 cup Water
DIPPING SAUCE
Soy sauce
Vinegar
Mushroom soaking liquid
-OR- water
Sesame oil (optional)
Chile oil (optional)
Make the filling by mashing the tofu to yield about 3/4 cup. Combine mashed tofu with the minced ingredients, salt, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Mix flour and water by hand until a ball of dough forms. Cover and let rest for at least an hour.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured board for 2 minutes or so. Roll it into a long, cylindrical shape, about 12 inches long and 1 inch in diameter. Cut crosswise into 1/2-inch pieces; you will have 24. If your climate is dry, keep the dough covered.
Shape these cut-side up on a flour-dusted board. Flatten them with the palm or heel of your hand. Roll each into a round wrapper, about 3 inches in diameter, thicker in the center and thinner toward the edge. Roll from the edge to the center and back again, turning the dough counterclockwise slightly with your left hand after each roll. Continue around several times, also turning the dough over once or twice.
Assemble by placing 1-1/2 teaspoons filling (or as much as the wrapper will hold) in an elongated mound in the center of each wrapper. Fold the dough over the filling so that the edges meet. Press the edges together for a tight seal, making four or five tiny pleats pinched tightly flush with the edge. Ensure it is completely sealed to keep the water out and the filling in.
Bring 4 cups water to a boil in a pot. Immerse eight dumplings at a time for 3 minutes (add an extra minute if frozen). Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. After 3 minutes, remove the dumplings with a slotted spoon. Cook the remaining two batches similarly. Serve hot with dipping sauces made of soy sauce and vinegar mixed in roughly equal proportions, thinned with water or mushroom liquid if needed. Add a drop of sesame oil and/or chile oil if desired. Some people like to add crushed garlic, minced green onion, and/or gingerroot.
Advance preparation: These can be assembled ahead and frozen. Do not defrost before cooking.
